   Anatomy of a Story Worksheet     

Story Name

McDonald’s PlayPlace

Thesis Statement

I once was a father who thought that his children were utterly dependent on me, but then some stuff happened, and I discovered that my children are far more capable than I ever thought. 



Chronological Structure 

ABC✔️

BAbC

CABC

Other:



 What are the stakes of the story? 

Stake #1

How will I get Charlie out of the tube?

Stake #2

How will my kids ever survive without me?

Stake #3







Start

Start with the Opposite of your ending





My children need me to teach them everything they will need to know and all the wisdom they will need to navigate this world.

Scene #1





Inside a McDonald’s Playplace. Charlie is in the tube. Petri dish of a location. Slowly reveal the identity of the boy.

Scene #2





Clara is eating pancakes because she only eats 5 foods. Not in tubes because everything is too loud, too tall, and too narrow for her.  

Emotional Goals

How do you want the audience to feel?

Amusement and wonder

Empathy for me and my suffering

Surprise

Is there a moment for surprise in this scene?

Who is the boy in the tube?



HumorDoes this scene have/require humor?

Define a McDonald’s Playplace in its worst terms

Clara’s limitations when it comes to eating. Flesh eating bacteria







Scene #3



Clara backstory:



Curious George moment

Pot & Pan Parade

NEED ONE MORE

Scene #4



Contrast to Charlie:



Puts everything in mouth

Never saw a door he didn’t want to walk thru

Never saw a corner he didn’t want to peek around



Brave, except…

Scene #5



    Charlie is stuck. 

    

    Clara won’t help. 

    

    Predictions of what I might need

    to do



















Empathy for me and my suffering

Relatability and amusement

Worry for me





Charlie is stuck. Make it seem silly but then real

Curious George and Pot & Pan Parade (both ridiculous, also contrast and define 

Phrasing of putting things in mouth

Predictions about failing father failing wife and children





Scene #6



   Clara agrees and climbs



   Gets stuck at cargo net

Scene #7



Clara reaches across. 



Whispers, “Whisper the name of the person you love most, and that will give you courage.

 

AMAZING.

Scene #8

Hope then hope dashed

Weepy exhilaration and disbelief



Clara doesn’t respond. She just starts moving. Sow doubt at each step. 

Clara’s statement



..

Not sure which name Charlie whispered but prob not mind









Scene #9



Scene #10



End

End your story with your 5-second moment.



Somehow, some way, my children have found knowledge and wisdom beyond my capacity… the kind of knowledge and wisdom that a person finds on their own. I think my kids will be okay.
